    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "Every bindable filter-field value (`nmp_grammar::Binding` mirror).\nRecursive through `FfiDerived`/`FfiSetOp` -- both are UniFFI **objects**\n(`Arc`-handles), not records: UniFFI's proc-macro mode lowers/lifts\n`Arc<T>` only when `T` is itself an exported object (an opaque handle),\nnever a plain `Box<T>`/`Arc<T>`-wrapped record -- and a record directly\ncontaining itself is a compile-time infinite-size error in Rust regardless\nof UniFFI. Making the recursive point an object (constructor + getters,\nsee each type's `impl`) is the standard UniFFI idiom for a recursive value\ntype; it mirrors `nmp_grammar::Binding`'s own `Box<Derived>`/`Box<SetOp>`\nindirection, just via an opaque handle instead of a boxed value. `SetOp`'s\n`operands: Vec<FfiBinding>` needs no such indirection -- `Vec` is already\nheap-allocated, breaking the cycle on its own.",
    ),
}

This is NOT a judgment that the string is a\nmalformed event tag (see [`Self::InvalidTag`] for that) -- a\nmulti-character or punctuation name is perfectly valid *event* data,\nit simply cannot be a generic filter key. `FfiSelector::Tag`'s `name`\nis never checked against this rule (#64).",

            name: "EngineClosed",
            discr: None,
            fields: [],
            docstring: Some(
                "[`nmp::Engine::shutdown`] has already run -- every other verb fails\nclosed with this variant instead of racing the engine thread's own\nteardown. NOTE: there is deliberately no `InvalidSignedEvent` variant\nhere anymore -- a `FfiWritePayload::Signed` that fails\n`nostr::Event::verify` is no longer rejected synchronously at this\nboundary (#52 Unit B). That guarantee moved to\n`nmp-engine::core::EngineCore::on_publish`'s acceptance boundary\n(Unit A0/#56) so it holds for every entry point, not only this one;\nit surfaces on the `WriteStatus` receipt stream as `Failed` instead.",
            ),

            discr: None,

                "#115: `NmpEngine::publish_composed` was called a second time on the\nsame `FfiComposedWriteIntent` handle -- it is take-once by design\n(recompose via `group_message_intent` again is the correct retry path,\nsince NMP-owned time and couriered `previous` evidence should refresh).",
            ),

                Field {
                    name: "original",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "source",
                    type_: Record {
                        module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                        name: "FfiSourceRange",
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,

                    name: "styles",

                        inner_type: Enum {
                            module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                            name: "FfiInlineStyle",

                    default: None,

            ],
            docstring: None,

        "A decoded public NIP-19 nostr entity (#116, `nmp::NostrEntity` mirror).\nEach variant carries EXACTLY the fields NIP-19 defines for that entity --\nnever force-fit into one shared shape: `npub`/`note` carry no relay\nhints at all (the format has none to carry); `nevent`'s `author`/`kind`\nare independently optional metadata; `naddr`'s `kind`/`author`/\n`identifier` are ALL required by the format, unlike `nevent`'s. There is\ndeliberately no `nsec`/`ncryptsec` variant here -- see\n`convert::decode_nostr_entity`'s doc for why a secret-key entity is\nrefused rather than decoded.",
    ),
}

enum Enum {
    name: "FfiWritePayload",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    discr_type: None,
    variants: [
        Variant {
            name: "Unsigned",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "pubkey",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "created_at",
                    type_: UInt64,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "kind",
                    type_: UInt16,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "tags",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: Sequence {
                            inner_type: String,
                        },
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "content",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "Signed",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "id",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "pubkey",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "created_at",
                    type_: UInt64,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "kind",
                    type_: UInt16,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "tags",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: Sequence {
                            inner_type: String,
                        },
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "content",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "sig",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "The event payload of a write intent (`nmp::WritePayload` mirror). VISION\nP: signing and publishing are ORTHOGONAL stages -- `Unsigned` is a\ntemplate the engine signs internally (\"the key lives in the engine\",\nledger #12); `Signed` (#32, the M5 unlock) is a caller that already\nholds a validly-signed event -- an external signer / NIP-46 bunker, or a\nverbatim republish -- and hands its fields across as-is. `Signed`'s\nfields are field-for-field [`FfiRow`] (the read-side mirror of a signed\n`nostr::Event`) plus `sig`, deliberately: the write side stays symmetric\nwith the read side rather than introducing a JSON-blob shape.\n\n`Signed`'s fields are PARSED at this FFI boundary (typed hex/signature-\nshape errors, see `convert::signed_event_from_ffi`) but NOT verified\nhere (#52 Unit B) -- `nostr::Event::verify` runs at\n`nmp-engine::core::EngineCore::on_publish`'s acceptance boundary (Unit\nA0/#56) instead, so the guarantee holds for every entry point, not only\nthis one. A tampered `Signed` event still parses fine here and is\nrejected downstream, surfacing as `WriteStatus::Failed` on the receipt\nstream rather than a synchronous `FfiError`. The engine itself never\nre-signs, mutates a tag, or recomputes an id for this variant.",
    ),
}

enum Enum {
    name: "FfiWriteRouting",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    discr_type: None,
    variants: [
        Variant {
            name: "AuthorOutbox",
            discr: None,
            fields: [],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "ToInboxes",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "recipients",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: String,
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "`nmp::WriteRouting` mirror -- `PrivateNarrow` deliberately has NO wire\nform here (#22/#52). `nmp_grammar::NarrowOnly::new`'s\nown doc requires \"the caller must already have resolved and narrowed\nthis itself\" -- i.e. a trusted protocol module's own logic, not a raw\nrelay-URL string handed across the FFI boundary by an app with no way to\nprove those URLs are actually private. Minting `PrivateRoute` from\nFFI-supplied strings would be exactly the \"raw app-provided expanded\nrelay set\"/\"route escape hatch\" #22's canonical design rules out; the\n`nmp` facade itself withholds re-exporting `NarrowOnly`/`PrivateRoute`\nfor the identical reason (see `crates/nmp/src/lib.rs`'s doc). A\nvalidated, opaque private-route mint belongs in a protocol module built\non direct Rust, not this FFI surface -- `AuthorOutbox`/`ToInboxes`\nremain the only FFI-constructible routing choices for now.\n\n#115: `PinnedHost(HostAuthority)` gets the IDENTICAL treatment, for the\nIDENTICAL reason -- this enum deliberately gains NO variant for it, and\nno `HostAuthority` constructor is exported anywhere in this crate. An\napp can only ever obtain a pinned-host write transitively, through a\nprotocol module's already-composed intent (`NmpEngine::group_message_intent`\n-> the opaque `FfiComposedWriteIntent`) -- never by naming a host\nitself. This exhaustive two-variant match IS the enforcement: a new\n`WriteRouting` variant landing in `nmp-grammar` without a corresponding\nFfiWriteRouting decision is a compile error here, not a silent gap.",
    ),
}

enum Enum {
    name: "FfiWriteStatus",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    discr_type: None,
    variants: [
        Variant {
            name: "Accepted",
            discr: None,
            fields: [],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "AwaitingCapability",
            discr: None,
            fields: [],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "Signed",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "event_id",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "Routed",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "relays",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: String,
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "Sent",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "relay",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,

            name: "Acked",

            docstring: None,

    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "The receipt STREAM (`nmp::WriteStatus` mirror; ledger #9 — enqueue is\nnot converged, the app's `ReceiptObserver` may see many of these per\npublish).",
    ),
}

            default: None,
            docstring: None,

        Field {
            name: "shortfall",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Enum {

                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(

    ),
}

        Field {
            name: "dropped_merge_rules",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "discovered_private_relays_rejected",
            type_: UInt64,
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "DISCOVERED relays rejected by the SSRF admission policy before they\ncould become routable lanes (issue #121): a network-sourced kind:10002\nnaming a loopback/RFC-1918/link-local/`.onion` host the operator never\nopted in. Counted PER LANE (write + read parse of one event), so a\nsingle hostile event naming `N` rejected hosts bumps this by up to\n`2N` — a rejection-event tally, not a distinct-host count.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "relays_rejected_over_cap",
            type_: UInt64,
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Relay dials the transport pool refused because the configured\n`max_relays` ceiling was already reached (issue #121, worker-exhaustion\ndefense). Always `0` when no cap is configured.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "transport_degraded",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Latest transport acceptance/verifier failure, if any. This is\nobservational diagnostics and never changes routing or trust policy.",
            ),
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "The engine-global diagnostics snapshot (M5 plan §1.1) -- \"the acceptance\ntest rendered on screen, permanently.\" Pushed reactively via\n`NmpEngine::observe_diagnostics`, never polled; read-only and off the\ndata path (never influences routing/delivery).",
    ),
}

This is the wire/local INDEXED filter alphabet\nonly (NIP-01 `#<letter>` queries) -- it is a distinct concept from\n[`FfiSelector::Tag`]'s arbitrary event-tag key, which is never restricted\nto a single letter.",
    ),
}

        Field {
            name: "relay",
            type_: String,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "wire_sub_count",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "authors_served",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "by_lane",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Record {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiLaneCount",
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "filters",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "The EXACT wire JSON of every filter currently sent to this relay\n(`ConcreteFilter::to_nostr().as_json()`, rendered engine-side).",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "events_by_kind",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Record {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiKindCount",
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "coverage",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Record {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiFilterCoverage",
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "One relay's full diagnostics (M5 plan §1.1) -- per-relay wire-sub count,\nexact filters, lane breakdown, reverse coverage (authors served), events\nactually received per kind, and per-filter coverage state. Every field\nhere is a REAL number read off the running engine -- never fabricated or\nestimated (the plan's truth-anchor rule).",
    ),
}

record Record {
    name: "FfiRow",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    fields: [
        Field {
            name: "id",
            type_: String,
            default: None,

        },

            name: "pubkey",
            type_: String,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },

            name: "created_at",
            type_: UInt64,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "kind",
            type_: UInt16,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,

            name: "tags",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Sequence {
                    inner_type: String,
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Each inner `Vec<String>` is one raw tag array (`[\"p\", \"<hex>\", ...]`),\nverbatim -- never parsed into a display-facing shape here.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "content",
            type_: String,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "sig",
            type_: String,
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "sources",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Sorted, deduplicated relay URLs that have delivered this event id.",
            ),
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(

    ),
}

record Record {
    name: "NmpEngineConfig",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    fields: [
        Field {
            name: "store_path",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "`None` -> in-memory store (nothing survives a restart). `Some(path)`\n-> a persistent `RedbStore` opened at that path (the same file\nreopened across restarts is what preserves source-scoped evidence for\na cold, offline read -- ledger #7).",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "indexer_relays",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "app_relays",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Operator app relay set (`Lane::AppRelay`). Default empty.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "fallback_relays",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Operator fallback relay set (`Lane::Fallback`). Default empty.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "allowed_local_relay_hosts",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: Some(
                EmptySequence,
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "Local/private relay HOSTS the operator explicitly opts into despite\nthe SSRF admission policy (issue #121). A DISCOVERED (network-sourced\nkind:10002) relay on a loopback / RFC-1918 / link-local / `.onion`\nhost is rejected by default; listing its host here (e.g. `\"127.0.0.1\"`\nor `\"localhost\"`) re-admits discovered relays on that exact host.\nHost-only match (port- and path-insensitive). Default empty.\n\n`default = []` keeps this field OPTIONAL for existing foreign-language\ncallers — adding it must not break records constructed before #121.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "max_relays",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: Some(
                UInt(
                    0,
                    Decimal,
                    UInt32,
                ),
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "OPT-IN, defense-in-depth ceiling on concurrently-connected relays\n(issue #121). `0` imposes no cap; a non-zero value refuses relay dials\npast it and counts them in the diagnostics `relays_rejected_over_cap`.\nNOT the primary worker-exhaustion defense — fan-out is already bounded\nby `nmp-router`'s solver cap; this is a coarse absolute backstop.\n\nThe `default =` literal below MUST stay equal to\n[`DEFAULT_MAX_RELAYS`] (uniffi record defaults accept only a literal,\nnever a const path) — the const is the single Rust-side knob; the\nliteral is its foreign-binding mirror. The default VALUE itself is an\nopen owner decision (sane cap vs. uncapped, issue #121); `0` is the\ninterim uncapped placeholder.",
            ),
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "Construction config for [`NmpEngine::new`]. See the module doc: the only\nrelay facts a caller ever supplies are the three operator-configured\nlanes -- `indexer_relays`, `app_relays`, `fallback_relays`\n(`routing-and-ownership.md` §2.1) -- everything else is discovered live.",
    ),
}

function Function {
    name: "group_content_demand",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    is_async: false,
    arguments: [
        Argument {
            name: "host",
            type_: String,
            by_ref: false,
            optional: false,
            default: None,
        },
        Argument {
            name: "group_id",
            type_: String,
            by_ref: false,
            optional: false,
            default: None,
        },
    ],
    return_type: Some(
        Record {
            module_path: "nmp_ffi",
            name: "FfiDemand",
        },
    ),
    ffi_func: FfiFunction {
        name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_func_group_content_demand",
        is_async: false,
        arguments: [
            FfiArgument {
                name: "host",
                type_: RustBuffer(
                    None,
                ),
            },
            FfiArgument {
                name: "group_id",
                type_: RustBuffer(
                    None,
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: Some(
            RustBuffer(
                Some(
                    ExternalFfiMetadata {
                        name: "FfiDemand",
                        module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    },
                ),
            ),
        ),
        has_rust_call_status_arg: true,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Group content (kinds 9, 30315), `h`-tag scoped to `group_id`, pinned to\n`host` (#108, `nmp_nip29::group_content_demand` mirror).",
    ),
    throws: Some(
        Enum {
            module_path: "nmp_ffi",

Opaque and generically named -- a future protocol\nmodule's own composed intent could reuse this same wrapper shape,\nnothing here is NIP-29-specific except how it's constructed.\nTake-once, not `Clone`/re-readable: [`NmpEngine::publish_composed`]\n(`crate::facade`) takes the inner intent exactly once, and a second\ncall fails closed with [`FfiError::IntentAlreadyConsumed`] rather than\nsilently re-publishing a stale template or handing back nothing.",
    ),
}

    docstring: Some(
        "Owns one remote-signer session. The native connection handle, not the\nengine, owns this value: `disconnect()`/drop therefore detach\ndeterministically instead of accumulating sessions until engine shutdown.\nConnection workers and callbacks retain only `Weak` references, avoiding\nboth an ownership cycle and a pending-handshake keepalive.",
    ),
}

    ],
    uniffi_traits: [],
    trait_impls: [],

A\ncaller-supplied `Signed` payload that fails verification is no\nlonger a synchronous error here (that guarantee moved to\n`nmp-engine::core::EngineCore::on_publish`'s acceptance boundary,\nUnit A0/#56, so it holds for every entry point, not only this one) --\nit surfaces as `WriteStatus::Failed`, the FIRST and only status\n`observer` receives, with no preceding `Accepted`.\nExhaustion of the pre-acceptance correlation namespace instead returns\na typed `FfiError` synchronously: no receipt id or stream exists.",

    ffi_init_callback: None,
    docstring: Some(
        "The app-facing handle to a live subscription (returned by\n[`NmpEngine::observe`]). `Drop` withdraws the subscription -- the SDK\nnever requires an app-owned container or lifecycle hook to make this\nhappen (plan §7's kill test). Holds ONLY the opaque\n[`nmp::ObservationCancel`] token (`Subscription::cancel_handle`) -- no\n`Handle`/`QueryHandle` (the raw imperative engine-control capability)\never reaches this crate. The receiving half of the subscription is owned\nentirely by [`NmpEngine::observe`]'s drain thread, since `recv()`\nblocks; `cancel()`/`Drop` here and the drain thread's own teardown\nconverge on the token's single withdrawal guard (see that type's doc).",
    ),
}

                55210,
            ),
        },
    ],

        name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_init_callback_vtable_diagnosticsobserver",
        is_async: false,

            FfiArgument {
                name: "vtable",
                type_: Reference(
                    Struct(
                        "VTableCallbackInterfaceDiagnosticsObserver",
                    ),
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: None,
        has_rust_call_status_arg: false,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Drains a live diagnostics stream (`nmp::Engine::observe_diagnostics`'s\n`DiagnosticsSubscription`, M5 plan §1.2 step 5). `on_snapshot` fires once\nper delivered snapshot, in order, on a dedicated drain thread — never on\nthe engine thread itself. Because the underlying mailbox is latest-wins\n(see `nmp::DiagnosticsSubscription::recv`'s doc), a slow Swift-side\nconsumer may observe fewer snapshots than were actually produced, but\nnever a stale one out of order. `on_closed` fires exactly once, when the\nobserver is cancelled or the engine shuts down.",
    ),
}

                    Struct(

                    ),
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: None,
        has_rust_call_status_arg: false,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },

}

        Method {
            name: "on_closed",
            object_name: "ReceiptObserver",
            object_module_path: "nmp_ffi",
            is_async: false,
            object_impl: Struct,
            arguments: [],
            return_type: None,
            ffi_func: FfiFunction {
                name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_method_receiptobserver_on_closed",
                is_async: false,
                arguments: [],
                return_type: None,
                has_rust_call_status_arg: true,
                is_object_free_function: false,
            },
            docstring: None,
            throws: None,
            takes_self_by_arc: false,
            checksum_fn_name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_checksum_method_receiptobserver_on_closed",
            checksum: Some(
                38112,
            ),
        },
    ],
    ffi_init_callback: FfiFunction {
        name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_init_callback_vtable_receiptobserver",
        is_async: false,
        arguments: [
            FfiArgument {
                name: "vtable",
                type_: Reference(
                    Struct(
                        "VTableCallbackInterfaceReceiptObserver",
                    ),
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: None,
        has_rust_call_status_arg: false,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Drains a publish's `Receiver<WriteStatus>` (ledger #9: enqueue is not\nconverged -- this may be called many times per publish, ending only when\nthe intent reaches every relay's terminal, or never at all for an\n`Ephemeral` intent, mirroring `Handle::publish`'s own receiver).\n`on_closed` fires exactly once, when the receipt's `Sender` is dropped\n(the intent has resolved or the engine has shut down) -- after which no\nfurther `on_status` call will ever occur, mirroring `RowObserver` and\n`DiagnosticsObserver`.",
    ),
}

callback CallbackInterface {
    name: "RowObserver",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    methods: [
        Method {
            name: "on_batch",
            object_name: "RowObserver",
            object_module_path: "nmp_ffi",
            is_async: false,
            object_impl: Struct,
            arguments: [
                Argument {
                    name: "deltas",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: Enum {
                            module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                            name: "FfiRowDelta",
                        },
                    },
                    by_ref: false,
                    optional: false,
                    default: None,
                },
                Argument {
                    name: "evidence",
                    type_: Record {
                        module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                        name: "FfiAcquisitionEvidence",
                    },
                    by_ref: false,
                    optional: false,
                    default: None,
                },
            ],
            return_type: None,
            ffi_func: FfiFunction {
                name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_method_rowobserver_on_batch",
                is_async: false,
                arguments: [],
                return_type: None,
                has_rust_call_status_arg: true,
                is_object_free_function: false,
            },
            docstring: None,
            throws: None,
            takes_self_by_arc: false,
            checksum_fn_name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_checksum_method_rowobserver_on_batch",
            checksum: Some(
                608,
            ),
        },
        Method {
            name: "on_closed",
            object_name: "RowObserver",
            object_module_path: "nmp_ffi",
            is_async: false,
            object_impl: Struct,
            arguments: [],
            return_type: None,
            ffi_func: FfiFunction {
                name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_method_rowobserver_on_closed",
                is_async: false,
                arguments: [],
                return_type: None,
                has_rust_call_status_arg: true,
                is_object_free_function: false,
            },
            docstring: None,
            throws: None,
            takes_self_by_arc: false,
            checksum_fn_name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_checksum_method_rowobserver_on_closed",
            checksum: Some(
                36135,
            ),
        },
    ],
    ffi_init_callback: FfiFunction {
        name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_init_callback_vtable_rowobserver",
        is_async: false,
        arguments: [
            FfiArgument {
                name: "vtable",
                type_: Reference(
                    Struct(
                        "VTableCallbackInterfaceRowObserver",
                    ),
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: None,
        has_rust_call_status_arg: false,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Drains a live subscription's `Receiver<RowsMsg>` (M4 §4b). `on_batch` is\ncalled once per delivered batch, in order, on a dedicated drain thread —\nnever on the engine thread itself, so a slow Swift-side consumer cannot\nstall `EngineCore`'s own recv loop. `on_closed` fires exactly once, when\nthe engine has torn the subscription down (cancel, or the row channel's\n`Sender` was dropped for any other reason) — after which no further\n`on_batch` call will ever occur. `evidence` is the query's scoped\nper-source acquisition evidence for this batch\n(`docs/design/scoped-evidence-49-12-plan.md` §4) -- never a collapsed\ncompleteness verdict.",
    ),
}
